reviews.review_title: Paid money then it became gambling slop
reviews.review_description: The transfer from paid to free-to-play made me insanely angry. I didn't even get anything for it. Along with f2p, it turned into a slots-themed gun skin game. Nobody plays the game anymore because of the sloppy bullcrap basically pushed in your face when you load in. I genuinely wish I would have GOTTEN something that was worth ANYTHING after the switch to f2p. This game SUCKS, DON'T waste your money

reviews.review_title: Good game but a few problems
reviews.review_description: The guns look pretty realistic, skins are cool, and you can find nice people. But, most of the people you will meet can be racist, underage, and not following rules. Now, other people talk about the problem how it used to cost money before it became free now, I got this game when it was free so I never had this problem, that’s why this is 4 stars. I do agree with those people though, but still, I never had to deal with that. In general, good game, rule breakers, and how the game used to cost money.

reviews.review_title: Needs to be fixed. Still fun, though.
reviews.review_description:
Pretty decent core gun mechanics. Other than that, this game is pretty questionable... Clunky object manipulation, low poly half-finished maps, etc. Although this game is free and likely in its earlier stages of development, don't expect an immersive or tactical atmosphere. You can still have fun though.
The real problems lie in how the game works. From the get-go, you have hardly any guns and attachments to choose from; only about one from each category. There are countless more, but they are all locked behind ranks. This means that players with higher ranks can easily outcompete players with lower ranks, creating a huge imbalance in PVP matches. In one match, I only had a few ranks and was restricted to basic, low power weapons. Meanwhile, an enemy with a high rank was dual wielding semi-automatic magazine fed shotguns and spawn killing me every 10 seconds. This is a big problem. Ranks are earned from XP, which is rewarded for completing objectives in multiplayer game modes, which brings me to the next topic: the community. Of course, being a free game, the community can't be good. But this community is simply awful. <13 year old kids yelling racial slurs, nonstop team killing, bullying, and outright unacceptable language. The worst part: there is NO way to kick a person, mute a person, disable team killing, or report a person. It's complete anarchy which is terrible for anyone who actually wants to play the game.

Needless to say, if you are looking for a game that is serious, tactical, immersive, realistic, or collaborative in any way, this one isn't for you. Get Tactical Assault VR instead. But, if you want to run around and shoot guns for free, I can't say that you won't have fun with this game. Just be wary.

The game was fun before the developers decided to make the game free. Now the people that had bought the game before it went free to play are out of that money, it would be nice if they gave us the founders pass with everything to make up for the waste of money.

Back in 2022, this was (I think) in early access. At the time, there were two options for playing the game: the demo and the full game. In the demo, the game only allowed you to use the weapons that we're on that table with the targets and such, and if you wanted to access more of the weapons, you'd pay up.

I paid about $17.47 for it. At the time, I thought this game could be the H3VR of Meta Quest platform since H3VR is (obviously) never going to be on here. It kept updating with more features here and there with cool tactical maps and things, and I kept playing the game up until I got bored with it and decided to move on to other games.

Then one day, I decided to see how the game was doing (I think around 2025?), come to find out that they made it free to play (kind of how Population: One went free to play). So I went to see how and what changed from the previous time I've played....

Things that have changed:

Weapons are now locked by ranks, which would've been fine if it weren't for the fact I can't even use them in Sandbox mode. In the older builds, you had all access to the weapons and didn't have to pay for a "Founders Pack," which is this games battle pass (that they keep showing almost every time).

Another thing that changed was the introduction of skins (I'm not sure if this was when the game went F2P* ((*free to play)) or they had skins beforehand), which again would've been fine had it not been the way you obtained them: via a slot machine (hey, if Counter Strike did it, why not this game?). There's also the option to trade with other players and whatnot for these skins, and you needed Lucky Tickets for it (yes, I know you can use shiny coins to buy the skins, but that's not my point) which if you wanted more could cost around 7$ (for 8) to over 27$ (for 60, which may or may not be that bad, idk).

There also came a map builder recently, but I didn't really care for it that much.

So, what's the point of this? If you are going to make your game free after a few years of making it a "paid for" experience, at least compensate for the people who bought it before. Why should I have to pay a 10$ subscription to be able to use the guns in a game I paid $17.47 for? And yes, while you can just play the game and rank to earn the weapons, I shouldn't have to do that in SANDBOX. I can't seem to contact the developer anywhere as their discord is wiped, and no one seems to care about it, so I'm just posting this here in case if anyone feels the same way about it.

Is it kinda bad that I wanted this game to be good? I mean, I guess I should've seen the signs of when they decided to use generative AI and the graphics and gameplay haven't been really all that polished.

If you don't care and just wanted to play, cool for ya. I'm not supporting the dev for the reasons above. If I can't get a refund because the game went F2P with the most scummy money-making practices I've ever seen, then I guess I won't be playing.

I'm not sure if this review will be seen by anyone, but if it does, then I hope the developer can see what many other people who paid for it before see.

If you're bored and have time to spare, this game is nice, good feel for the guns, customization is great, but the game has many griefers, actual briefing, not just some kid killing you once or twice. Also, the game can get VERY laggy randomly.

reviews.review_title: Horrible decision making ruined the game
reviews.review_description: The decision to lock every gun behind a level system ruined this game. I bought and played this game because it was fun to experiment with whatever gun I want. Now I would have to play for an unreasonable amount of time or purchase micro-transactions just for a chance to get one gun. I didn’t even get anything for buying the game in early access. I probably won’t play this game again unless they reverse this decision.

Most lobbies you join are filled with immature 10yr olds that curse you out for anything, but the micro transactions.. I don't have to say anything if you load up the game and view the lobby. But I do like how practically everything is free in this if you just grind! Overall, this game needs some fixes and workarounds

No clue why they did this but the thing thats the most crazy is the fact that this paywall still applies to when you play sandbox mode! Yes they will still try to get you to spend money on that pass go literally use guns in a SANDBOX mode… its a fun game but it seems like the dev’s are looking more towards the money than their people…

reviews.review_title: Solid for a free to play game
reviews.review_description: I think this game is great. The guns are pretty realistic, with some of them even having different firing modes. The physics are fine, but sometimes my weapon clips into something or my hands get stuck somewhere. The multiplayer is really fun to play, but sometimes there are annoying people always trying to kill you.The graphics are what you would expect from a free game, but the guns are pretty detailed. Overall this is a good free to play game, especially if you play with friends.
